bug_report.summary: After saving a new Markdown file, the new document is not opened/focused in the main editor; focus returns to an existing note
steps_to_reproduce: Choose Markdown and click Save (leave the filename as default/untitled).
Observe which document is active in the editor after the save dialog closes.
expected_behaviors: The newly created Markdown document should become the active document for editing immediately after saving (cursor in the new empty document).
actual_behaviors: After saving, the editor shows the content of an existing document (“Welcome to Zettlr!”) instead of the new empty file; the status bar/word count reflects the existing document rather than a new 0-word document (diffs include #2, #10, #12, and many content-region changes).

bug_report.summary: Pandoc-style fenced code block header ```{.nix} appears to trigger syntax highlighting changes (likely Nix highlighting) when it should remain unhighlighted
expected_behaviors: Pandoc-style fence header `{.nix}` should not be treated as a language selector; code block should remain with 'No highlighting' (no Nix syntax highlighting applied).
actual_behaviors: Rendering/styling differs in the `{.nix}` fence header token and within the code body (token/string coloring changes), consistent with the code block being parsed/highlighted differently (potentially as Nix) despite using Pandoc-style `{.nix}`.

bug_report.summary: Word/character counters show 0 after typing content into the new document
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown file via File → New file…, then Save.
Click into the editor area for that document and type the provided Python fenced code block.
Check the word/character counts in the UI (top-right word count and/or the pane status bar).
expected_behaviors: After typing, word/character counters should reflect the newly inserted content (non-zero character count at minimum).
actual_behaviors: In the after-change build, counters remain at “0 words” / “0 characters” despite the code block being visible in the document pane, indicating the counters are not updating or are calculating incorrectly.
